summary: Russian Tu-22M3 bombers launched from Olenya airfield, and Shahed drones flew toward Kyiv, indicating heightened long‑range strike activity. Russian forces reported liberating dozens of buildings in Konstantinovka and Krasny Liman, with significant Ukrainian casualties and equipment losses. Multiple guided bomb and UAV attacks hit civilian infrastructure in Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and the Black Sea.
key_points: Three Tu-22M3 bombers took off from Olenya airfield, with three more scheduled to launch later (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67903).
A Shahed drone was observed crossing from Chernihiv region toward Kyiv/Boryspil (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67901).
Russian forces claimed to have liberated 627 buildings in Konstantinovka, killing over 540 Ukrainian troops and destroying 160 weapons and 116 vehicles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Guided bombs were launched by Russian tactical aviation on Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
A Russian guided aerial bomb hit Kharkiv, injuring nine people including four children and damaging over forty houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82042).
Russian drones attacked civilian vessels in the Black Sea, killing one crew member and wounding several others (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
A Russian drone struck a minibus in the Korablevny district of Kherson, causing at least four injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82043).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑eastern front, warning of possible strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/65587).

sections.executive_summary: Russian Tu-22M3 bombers launched from Olenya airfield and a Shahed drone headed toward Kyiv, showing increased long‑range strike capability. Russian forces reported liberating hundreds of buildings in Konstantinovka and Krasny Liman, with heavy Ukrainian personnel and equipment losses. Multiple guided bomb and UAV attacks struck civilian targets in Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk and the Black Sea, raising the risk of further civilian casualties.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported nine injured, including four children, after a Russian guided aerial bomb hit Kharkiv, damaging more than forty private homes (Source: @UkraineNow/82042).

In the Black Sea, a Russian drone strike on a civilian vessel killed one crew member and wounded several others, including crew from ships flying the Panamanian and St. Kitts & Nevis flags (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
A Russian drone attack on a minibus in Kherson's Korablevny district resulted in at least four injured civilians, three men and one woman (Source: @UkraineNow/82043).
Ukrainian tactical units reported multiple UAV incursions across northern and central regions, including Shahed drones moving from Chernihiv toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67901).

The Russian Defense Ministry announced the liberation of 627 buildings in Konstantinovka, claiming over 540 Ukrainian troops killed and 160 weapons plus 116 vehicles destroyed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
In Krasny Liman, Russian forces said they seized 37 strongholds and 251 buildings, reporting 185 Ukrainian casualties and loss of 45 weapons and equipment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, targeting infrastructure and frontline positions (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Russian forces reported striking a fuel tanker park, though details on casualties or damage were not provided (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31017).

In Konstantinovka, Russian assault groups continued offensive actions in the southwestern part of the settlement and the Nikolayevsky district, reporting the destruction of over 540 Ukrainian troops and extensive equipment losses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31023).
Krasny Liman saw Russian troops seize dozens of strongholds, with reported Ukrainian losses of 185 personnel and 45 weapons systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted in Sumy region, with at least five drones operating near Shostka (Source: @eRadarrua/97243).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned on the north‑eastern direction, indicating potential strikes on frontline areas in that sector (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
A Shahed drone was tracked crossing from Chernihiv region toward Kyiv and Boryspil, highlighting a northern approach vector (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67901).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAVs heading from northern Sumy toward Shostka and from northern Kharkiv toward Lozova (Source: @kpszsu/65558).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑eastern front, suggesting possible upcoming strikes (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
Ukrainian forces noted a UAV heading from northern Sumy toward Shostka, indicating continued aerial reconnaissance in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/97271).
Russian drone attacks on civilian vessels in the Black Sea resulted in one fatality and multiple injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
A Russian drone struck a minibus in Kherson's Korablevny district, injuring four civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82043).
Ukrainian reports listed UAV activity in southern Mykolaiv region heading toward Koblev (Source: @kpszsu/65558).
Guided bomb strikes by Russian tactical aviation were reported in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, affecting southern front positions (Source: @kpszsu/65597).
Three Tu-22M3 strategic bombers took off from Olenya airfield, with three additional bombers scheduled for launch, indicating a surge in long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67903).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, targeting both military and civilian infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
Multiple UAV incursions were reported across Ukraine, including Shahed drones from the north and various reconnaissance drones in Sumy, Kharkiv, and Mykolaiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/65558).
Russian air activity on the north‑eastern front was highlighted as a threat, with warnings of possible aerial strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/65587).

key_developments: Three Tu-22M3 bombers launched from Olenya airfield, with three more scheduled, expanding Russia's strategic strike reach (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67903).
A Shahed drone was observed crossing from Chernihiv toward Kyiv/Boryspil, indicating a northern aerial threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67901).
Russian forces claimed liberation of 627 buildings in Konstantinovka, reporting over 540 Ukrainian casualties and loss of 160 weapons and 116 vehicles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Guided bomb attacks by Russian tactical aviation hit Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions, targeting infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/65592).
A Russian guided bomb struck Kharkiv, injuring nine civilians, including four children, and damaging over forty houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82042).
Russian drones attacked civilian vessels in the Black Sea, killing one crew member and wounding several others (Source: @UkraineNow/82044).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned on the north‑eastern front, suggesting imminent aerial strikes on frontline positions (Source: @kpszsu/65587).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian long‑range strike assets, notably Tu-22M3 bombers, are being employed from Olenya, increasing pressure on deep Ukrainian targets. Simultaneously, the proliferation of Shahed drones and guided bomb attacks across multiple fronts raises the risk of civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Russian gains in Konstantinovka and Krasny Liman reflect continued offensive momentum, while heightened UAV activity suggests persistent reconnaissance and harassment capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defense around Kyiv and key southern ports following drone incursions and missile strikes. Local emergency services are responding to civilian injuries in Kharkiv, Kherson, and Black Sea incidents, while military units are reallocating assets to counter guided bomb threats in Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk. Coordination with NATO partners on air defense integration is likely to intensify.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Tu-22M3 sortie patterns from Olenya is critical for early warning of potential deep strikes. Shahed drone flight paths from Chernihiv to Kyiv warrant enhanced radar coverage and interceptor readiness. The reported Russian liberation of urban areas in the east suggests a focus on consolidating territorial gains, necessitating close observation of supply line movements and civilian displacement trends.
